name: Garnett Brown
abstract: Garnett Pompilius Brown (January 31, 1936 – October 9, 2021) was an American jazz trombonist who worked with The Crusaders, Herbie Hancock, Lionel Hampton, Earth Wind and Fire and others. Born in Memphis, Tennessee, he graduated from the University of Arkansas at Pine Bluff and later studied film scoring and electronic music at UCLA.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: at first, it was Garnett Brown who called me, the trombonist. I worked with him for about a year... Garnett Brown and myself... Garnett Brown dropped out... Garnett Brown he said, he told everybody
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Lloyd
about_quotes.evidence: That’s when I brought in Gabor and Stinson and Garnett Brown whom I had gone to high school with in Memphis... So I brought Garnett in.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Lloyd — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Daniels, Garnett Brown on trombone, myself. And we were the young musicians in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: Garnett Brown, another one of my home towners, great trombone player, unsung hero... He did some orchestrations for Herbie. He did the soundtrack for a movie called “Harlem Nights”... He went to school with Reggie Miller’s father
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Oliver Nelson Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: I mean Garnett Brown on trombone... So that was just kind of his normal set of people to play his music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Oliver Nelson Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 10, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Wesley
about_quotes.evidence: Garnett Brown was already out there you know. So there wasn’t much call for another jazz trombone player
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Wesley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 21, 2024
